title: The Coming Of Good Luck
content:
So Good-Luck came, and on my roof did light,
Like noiseless snow, or as the dew of night;
Not all at once, but gently, as the trees
Are by the sun-beams, tickled by degrees.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "The Coming Of Good Luck"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
